enrichment.short_description: Flexible employee benefits platform for Lifestyle Spending Accounts, HSAs, FSAs, and HRAs.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Forma Benefits lets employers offer flexible LSA, HSA, FSA, and HRA spending accounts employees can use on benefits that fit their needs.

features.title: Lifestyle Spending Accounts (LSAs)
features.description: Employers fund customizable accounts employees can spend on wellness, family, and lifestyle benefits.

features.title: Pre-tax accounts
features.description: Supports HSA and FSA account types alongside lifestyle spending options.

features.title: Health Reimbursement Arrangements
features.description: HRAs let employers reimburse employees for qualifying health expenses.

features.title: Forma Store
features.description: A curated marketplace of vendors employees can use their allocated funds with.

features.title: Forma Visa Card
features.description: A card that lets employees pay for eligible benefits without fronting out-of-pocket costs.

features.title: Claims administration
features.description: Handles claims processing and reimbursement for account-based benefits.

features.title: Analytics dashboard
features.description: Gives benefits teams reporting and utilization data across programs.

pros: Combines LSAs, HSAs, FSAs, and HRAs in a single platform
Forma Visa Card removes the need for employees to pay out of pocket
Curated vendor marketplace (Forma Store) simplifies spending eligible funds
Reports 87% annual utilization rate and 98% five-year member satisfaction
Available on web, iOS, and Android
cons: No public pricing; requires a demo or consultation
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site
Specific integration partners are not listed publicly

faqs.question: What account types does Forma support?
faqs.answer: Forma supports Lifestyle Spending Accounts (LSAs), pre-tax HSAs and FSAs, and Health Reimbursement Arrangements (HRAs).

faqs.question: Is there a card for spending Forma funds?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Forma Visa Card lets employees pay for eligible benefits directly without paying out of pocket first.

faqs.question: How is Forma priced?
faqs.answer: Forma does not publish pricing; companies need to request a demo for a quote.

faqs.question: Is Forma available on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes, Forma offers both iOS and Android apps in addition to its web platform.

use_cases.title: Flexible benefits program design
use_cases.description: HR and benefits teams build a customizable benefits program instead of a one-size-fits-all package.

use_cases.title: Global lifestyle spending
use_cases.description: Companies offer lifestyle spending accounts that support benefits needs across a distributed or global workforce.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Forma Benefits?
paragraphs: Forma Benefits is an employee benefits platform that lets organizations offer Lifestyle Spending Accounts (LSAs), pre-tax HSAs and FSAs, and Health Reimbursement Arrangements (HRAs) so employees can choose benefits that match their individual needs.

The platform includes the Forma Store, a curated vendor marketplace, and the Forma Visa Card, which lets employees use allocated funds directly instead of paying out of pocket and seeking reimbursement.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Forma Benefits is for
paragraphs: Forma is built for benefits professionals and HR teams that want to move beyond a fixed benefits package and offer employees more flexibility in how they spend allocated benefits funds, including companies with global or distributed workforces.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered compensation management platform with real-time benchmarking from 9,000+ companies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pave is a compensation management platform offering free and paid salary benchmarking, market pricing, and merit-cycle planning tools.

features.title: Real-time compensation benchmarks
features.description: Draws data from 9,000+ companies across 200+ job families and 16 business functions.

features.title: AI-assisted job matching
features.description: Uses machine learning to match roles to benchmark data for more accurate pricing.

features.title: Market pricing and salary bands
features.description: Tools for building and maintaining salary ranges.

features.title: Compensation planning workflows
features.description: Supports merit cycle management and budget-based planning.

features.title: Total Rewards Portal
features.description: A portal for communicating total compensation to employees.

features.title: Visual Offer Letter
features.description: Generates visual offer letters for candidates.

features.title: Team View dashboards
features.description: Gives managers visibility into team compensation.

pros: Free Market Data Lite tier for companies with 1-200 employees
Benchmark data sourced from 9,000+ companies
Covers 55+ countries and 90+ cities/metros on the Pro tier
AI-assisted job matching for benchmark accuracy
Unlimited user seats, search queries, and report downloads on all plans
cons: Market Data Pro and Full Suite pricing require booking a demo (not public)
Free tier is limited to U.S. market plus one additional market
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Pave have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Market Data Lite is free for companies with 1-200 employees and includes base salary and new-hire equity benchmarks.

faqs.question: How many companies contribute data to Pave's benchmarks?
faqs.answer: Pave sources real-time data from 9,000+ companies.

faqs.question: What does Market Data Pro add over the free tier?
faqs.answer: Market Data Pro adds access to 55+ countries and 90+ cities/metros, advanced equity insights, geo-differentials, peer groups, and custom report building.

faqs.question: Does Pave use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pave uses AI-assisted job matching and machine learning to power predictive compensation insights.

use_cases.title: Startup salary benchmarking
use_cases.description: Small companies use the free Market Data Lite tier to benchmark base salary and equity for new hires.

use_cases.title: Enterprise compensation planning
use_cases.description: Larger organizations use Market Data Pro and Workflows to run merit cycles, build pay ranges, and communicate total rewards.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pave?
paragraphs: Pave is a compensation management platform that combines real-time market benchmarking, AI-assisted job matching, and compensation planning workflows. It sources data from more than 9,000 companies to help organizations set and communicate pay.
The platform ranges from a free Market Data Lite tier for small companies to a Full Suite that adds market pricing, planning workflows, and a Total Rewards Portal for employee communication.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pave is for
paragraphs: Pave serves compensation professionals, HR leaders, and total rewards teams at organizations ranging from early-stage startups to large global enterprises.

enrichment.short_description: Compensation management software for salary benchmarking, salary bands, and collaborative pay reviews.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Figures helps HR and managers benchmark pay, build salary bands, run compensation reviews, and analyze pay equity with real-time market data.

features.title: Benchmark
features.description: Access to 3,500,000+ compensation datapoints for salary comparison.

features.title: Salary Bands
features.description: Structure pay grids as a documented source of truth for compensation ranges.

features.title: Compensation Review
features.description: Collaborative workflows for annual and off-cycle reviews with budget tracking.

features.title: Pay Equity Analysis
features.description: Identifies and helps address pay gaps with data-backed insights.

features.title: Compliance tools
features.description: Supports requirements under the EU Pay Transparency Directive.

features.title: HRIS integrations
features.description: Connects with 30+ HRIS and review systems.

pros: 3,500,000+ benchmark datapoints
Used by 10,000+ users according to the company
Notable customers include Doctolib, KPMG, TotalEnergies, EY, Qonto, and Swan
Built-in support for EU Pay Transparency Directive compliance
Integrates with 30+ HRIS and review systems
cons: Pricing is not public and varies by employee count and modules selected
Requires a demo to get a quote
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How is Figures priced?
faqs.answer: Figures pricing is based on the number of employees in your company and varies according to the modules you select; a personalized quote requires booking a demo.

faqs.question: What compensation data does Figures provide?
faqs.answer: Figures gives access to more than 3,500,000 benchmark datapoints for comparing salaries against the market.

faqs.question: Does Figures support pay equity requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, Figures includes pay equity analysis and compliance tools supporting the EU Pay Transparency Directive.

faqs.question: What plans does Figures offer?
faqs.answer: Figures offers Structure, Companion, and a standalone Pay Transparency module, plus an optional Pay Equity Expert add-on.

use_cases.title: Building salary bands
use_cases.description: Compensation teams use Figures to build pay ranges backed by market benchmark data.

use_cases.title: Pay equity compliance
use_cases.description: Organizations use Figures' pay equity analysis to prepare for EU Pay Transparency Directive requirements.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Figures?
paragraphs: Figures is compensation management software that gives HR teams and managers real-time market benchmarks, dynamic salary bands, and collaborative tools for running pay reviews.
The platform includes pay equity analysis and compliance features aligned with the EU Pay Transparency Directive, and connects with 30+ HRIS and performance review systems.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Figures is for
paragraphs: Figures is built for HR and compensation teams that need to collaborate with managers on pay decisions, particularly organizations subject to European pay transparency requirements.

enrichment.short_description: Real-time compensation benchmarking and management platform for global tech companies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ravio provides real-time compensation benchmarking, salary bands, and pay equity tools sourced from 1,400+ companies across 50+ countries.

features.title: Real-time compensation benchmarking
features.description: Live salary and total rewards data sourced from HR systems of 1,400+ companies across 50+ countries.

features.title: Salary bands
features.description: Build and maintain pay ranges backed by market data.

features.title: Pay equity analysis
features.description: Tools to review and address pay equity across the organization.

features.title: Compensation reviews
features.description: Workflow support for running compensation review cycles.

features.title: HRIS integrations
features.description: Connects with 75+ HR systems, including HiBob, Workday, and Personio, to sync job, salary, and employee data automatically.

features.title: Filterable benchmark data
features.description: Filter comparison data by location, industry, funding stage, and headcount.

pros: Live data sourced directly from HR systems of 1,400+ companies
Covers 50+ countries and 300+ roles
Connects with 75+ HR systems including HiBob, Workday, and Personio
Backed by $22M in total funding including a $12M Series A led by Spark Capital
cons: Annual plans start at £5,000, which may be significant for very small companies
Company size and detailed AI capabilities are not disclosed on the site
Targeted primarily at high-growth tech companies, so vertical fit may vary

faqs.question: When was Ravio founded?
faqs.answer: Ravio was founded in 2022.

faqs.question: Where is Ravio headquartered?
faqs.answer: Ravio is headquartered in London, United Kingdom.

faqs.question: How much does Ravio cost?
faqs.answer: Ravio's annual plans start from £5,000.

faqs.question: What HR systems does Ravio integrate with?
faqs.answer: Ravio integrates with 75+ HR systems, including HiBob, Workday, and Personio.

use_cases.title: Real-time salary benchmarking
use_cases.description: Compensation teams compare internal pay against live market data across countries and roles.
use_cases.audience: Compensation and HR teams at tech companies

use_cases.title: Building pay equity into reviews
use_cases.description: Teams use Ravio's pay equity analysis during compensation review cycles.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ravio?
paragraphs: Ravio is a compensation management platform founded in 2022 and headquartered in London. It provides real-time salary and total rewards benchmarking sourced directly from the HR systems of 1,400+ companies across 50+ countries.
Beyond benchmarking, Ravio includes salary bands, pay equity analysis, and compensation review workflows, and integrates with 75+ HR systems including HiBob, Workday, and Personio.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ravio is for
paragraphs: Ravio is designed for high-growth tech and tech-enabled companies that need live, verified compensation data to manage pay across multiple countries.

enrichment.short_description: AI compensation intelligence platform giving enterprise teams live market data and AI agents for pay decisions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compa gives enterprise compensation teams AI agents and live market data across offers, employees, stock, and skills for pay decisions.

features.title: Analyst Agent
features.description: An AI agent that automates compensation analysis for internal teams.

features.title: Partner Agent
features.description: An AI agent that helps guide external-facing compensation decisions.

features.title: Live market data
features.description: Tracks 9M+ observations including 1.2M+ offers, 4.8M+ employees, and 1.2M+ stock grants across 42+ countries.

features.title: Compa Connect
features.description: Integrates compensation intelligence into Slack, Claude, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, and Gemini.

features.title: ATS and stock system integrations
features.description: Connects with systems including Workday, Oracle, Greenhouse, Carta, and E*TRADE.

pros: Tracks 9M+ live compensation observations across 42+ countries
AI agents (Analyst Agent, Partner Agent) automate analysis and decision guidance
Compa Connect brings compensation data into Slack, ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, and Gemini
Integrates with major ATS and equity systems like Workday, Oracle, Greenhouse, and Carta
cons: Market Data starts at $35,000/year, positioning it for enterprise budgets
Agents pricing requires contacting sales
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Compa cost?
faqs.answer: Compa's Market Data plan starts at $35,000/year; pricing for the Agents plan requires contacting sales.

faqs.question: What AI agents does Compa offer?
faqs.answer: Compa offers an Analyst Agent for internal compensation analysis and a Partner Agent for external-facing decision guidance.

faqs.question: Which tools does Compa Connect work with?
faqs.answer: Compa Connect integrates with Slack, Claude, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, and Gemini.

faqs.question: What systems does Compa integrate with?
faqs.answer: Compa integrates with ATS and stock systems including Workday, Oracle, Greenhouse, Carta, and E*TRADE.

use_cases.title: Enterprise compensation analysis
use_cases.description: Compensation teams use the Analyst Agent to automate analysis of offers and pay data.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted pay decisions in existing tools
use_cases.description: Teams query compensation data directly from Slack, ChatGPT, or Copilot via Compa Connect.

features: Employees, Peer Groups, Geo Tiers, Location Insights, Leveling Insights, Research
Optional add-ons: Offers, Stock, Frontline, Skills

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Compa?
paragraphs: Compa is an AI-powered compensation intelligence platform for enterprise teams. It combines live market data—tracking more than 9 million observations across offers, employees, stock grants, and skills in 42+ countries—with AI agents that automate compensation analysis.
Through Compa Connect, the platform's data and agents can be accessed directly inside Slack, Claude, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, and Gemini.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Compa is for
paragraphs: Compa is built for enterprise compensation and talent acquisition teams that need live market data and AI-assisted analysis integrated into the systems they already use, such as Workday, Oracle, Greenhouse, and Carta.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered people analytics and workforce planning platform starting at $5 per employee per month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ChartHop is a people analytics platform with org charts, headcount planning, compensation, and performance modules from $5/employee/month.

features.title: Headcount Planning
features.description: Visual org planning tools for HR, finance, and managers.

features.title: HRIS
features.description: Centralizes employee data and org chart management.

features.title: Compensation
features.description: Pay cycle management with budget-aware workflows.

features.title: Performance
features.description: Integrated performance review cycles.

features.title: Engagement
features.description: Pulse surveys and sentiment analysis.

features.title: Goals
features.description: Real-time goal visibility aligned to business data.

features.title: ChartHop AI (Ask ChartHop)
features.description: Conversational AI that answers questions, fills forms, models org changes, and automates repeatable tasks using people and business data.

pros: Modular pricing starting at $5/employee/month for the Core plan
Connects with 100+ HRIS, ATS, and FP&A tools including ADP, Workday, Greenhouse, and BambooHR
Ask ChartHop AI can model org changes and automate workflows
Rated 4.3/5 on G2 based on 150+ reviews (Winter 2026)
Named a G2 Leader and High Performer for Winter 2026
cons: Each additional module (HRIS, Headcount Planning, Compensation, Performance) costs extra per employee per month
ChartHop AI Pro is billed pay-as-you-go on top of module pricing
Enterprise pricing for large organizations is custom and not public
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the pricing page

faqs.question: How is ChartHop priced?
faqs.answer: ChartHop Core starts at $5 per employee per month, with add-on modules like HRIS, Headcount Planning, and Compensation priced at $3-$4 per employee per month, billed annually.

faqs.question: Does ChartHop have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, ChartHop AI Pro (Ask ChartHop) provides conversational AI that can answer questions, model org changes, and automate tasks, billed pay-as-you-go.

faqs.question: What systems does ChartHop integrate with?
faqs.answer: ChartHop connects with 100+ HRIS, ATS, and FP&A tools, including ADP, Workday, Greenhouse, SAP SuccessFactors, Gusto, BambooHR, and Lattice.

faqs.question: What is ChartHop's G2 rating?
faqs.answer: ChartHop holds a 4.3 out of 5 rating on G2 based on more than 150 reviews as of Winter 2026.

use_cases.title: Headcount and budget planning
use_cases.description: HR and finance teams jointly plan headcount changes with budget visibility.

use_cases.title: Compensation cycle management
use_cases.description: Compensation teams run merit and promotion cycles with real-time budget tracking.

use_cases.title: Conversational people analytics
use_cases.description: Managers and executives ask Ask ChartHop natural-language questions about workforce data instead of building reports manually.

pricing_plans.plan_name: Add-on modules (HRIS, Headcount Planning, Compensation, Performance)

features: For organizations managing thousands of employees across multiple entities

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ChartHop?
paragraphs: ChartHop is a people analytics and workforce planning platform that combines people, financial, and business data into modules for headcount planning, HRIS, compensation, performance, engagement, and goals.

Pricing is modular: the Core plan starts at $5 per employee per month, with additional modules priced separately, and Ask ChartHop AI automates tasks and answers natural-language questions about workforce data.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ChartHop is for
paragraphs: ChartHop serves people leaders, finance/FP&A teams, managers, and talent acquisition teams at mid-market to enterprise organizations that want to run workforce decisions from a single data platform rather than static reports.

enrichment.short_description: Enterprise organizational design and workforce planning platform, founded in 2005 and based in London.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Orgvue is an organizational design and workforce planning platform for enterprises, now adding Henshaw AI capabilities.

features.title: Organizational design
features.description: Tools to analyze and redesign organizational structures.

features.title: Workforce planning
features.description: Modeling and forecasting of workforce structures and skills.

features.title: Data import and harmonization
features.description: Connects and unifies workforce data from multiple sources.

features.title: Predictive modeling
features.description: Forecasts and monitors organizational changes over time.

features.title: Henshaw Assistant
features.description: A conversational, natural-language AI interface (early access) that lets HR and workforce planning leaders ask questions about their data.

features.title: Henshaw Roles
features.description: An AI capability (early access) that is part of the Henshaw suite for workforce design.

pros: Established platform founded in 2005 with an enterprise focus
Introducing Henshaw AI suite for natural-language workforce planning queries
Supports large-scale organizational and workforce transformation projects
Applies dedicated LLMs and stated data security/compliance procedures for AI features
cons: Pricing is entirely custom-quote based, with nothing public
Henshaw AI features (Assistant, Roles) are in early access, not generally available
Company size is not disclosed

faqs.question: When was Orgvue founded?
faqs.answer: Orgvue was founded in 2005.

faqs.question: Where is Orgvue headquartered?
faqs.answer: Orgvue is headquartered in London, United Kingdom.

faqs.question: Does Orgvue have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Orgvue introduced the Henshaw AI suite, including Henshaw Assistant and Henshaw Roles, currently available through an early access program.

faqs.question: How much does Orgvue cost?
faqs.answer: Orgvue does not publish pricing; it is provided on a custom-quote basis tailored to enterprise needs.

use_cases.title: Organizational redesign
use_cases.description: Large organizations use Orgvue to analyze and redesign reporting structures and roles.

use_cases.title: Workforce transformation planning
use_cases.description: Teams model and forecast workforce changes during M&A or restructuring.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Orgvue?
paragraphs: Orgvue is a cloud-based organizational design and workforce planning platform, founded in 2005 and headquartered in London. It lets enterprises import, visualize, model, and monitor workforce structures and skills.
The company has begun rolling out a Henshaw AI suite, including a conversational Henshaw Assistant and Henshaw Roles capability, currently available to customers through an early access program.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Orgvue is for
paragraphs: Orgvue is designed for large enterprises and global organizations undertaking organizational design, workforce transformation, or M&A-related restructuring.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based org chart and employee directory software, now part of the Workleap employee experience suite.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pingboard, now part of Workleap, provides dynamic org charts and an employee directory for hybrid and remote teams.

features.title: Dynamic org charts
features.description: Visualizes reporting structures, departments, and cross-functional teams that update automatically.

features.title: Employee directory
features.description: Detailed view of employee roles, departments, and team composition.

features.title: Custom profiles
features.description: Displays colleague interests and skills to support collaboration.

features.title: Who's Who game
features.description: An interactive face-to-name matching activity for team engagement.

features.title: Planning org charts
features.description: Private charts for scenario planning around resource allocation, with commenting and feedback.

features.title: HRIS data sync
features.description: Imports employee data via automated HRIS integrations or Excel upload.

pros: Founded in 2013 with an established org chart product
Now backed by Workleap's broader employee experience ecosystem
Used by over 70,000 leaders worldwide across Workleap's product suite
Supports both automated HRIS sync and manual Excel import
cons: Acquired by Workleap; pricing and product direction now reflect Workleap's broader suite rather than a standalone company
Historical published pricing (from $149/month for 20 users) may not reflect current Workleap pricing
No AI capabilities are mentioned for the product

faqs.question: When was Pingboard founded?
faqs.answer: Pingboard was founded in 2013 by Rob Eanes and Bill Boebel.

faqs.question: Is Pingboard still an independent company?
faqs.answer: No, Pingboard is now part of Workleap and operates as one of its employee experience products, still under the Pingboard name.

faqs.question: What does Pingboard's org chart tool do?
faqs.answer: It creates dynamic, automatically updating org charts showing reporting lines, departments, and cross-functional teams, built from HRIS data or an Excel upload.

faqs.question: Does Pingboard have an employee directory?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pingboard includes an employee directory with custom profiles showing roles, departments, and team composition.

use_cases.title: Visualizing company structure
use_cases.description: HR teams give remote and hybrid employees visibility into reporting lines and team composition.

use_cases.title: Workforce scenario planning
use_cases.description: Leaders use private planning org charts to model resource allocation changes before rolling them out.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pingboard?
paragraphs: Pingboard is a cloud-based org chart and employee directory tool founded in 2013 and headquartered in Austin, Texas. It gives distributed and hybrid organizations dynamic visibility into reporting structures and team composition.
Pingboard has been acquired and now operates as part of Workleap's employee experience product suite, still under the Pingboard name, alongside Workleap's other engagement and recognition tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pingboard is for
paragraphs: Pingboard is used by HR and People Ops teams managing remote or hybrid workforces who need an up-to-date org chart and employee directory synced from their HRIS.

enrichment.short_description: Organizational visibility platform for automated org charts, employee directories, and people search, based in Detroit.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sift Org Chart provides automated org charts, employee directories, and people search with a 14-day free trial, from $150/month.

features.title: Automated org charts
features.description: Builds org charts from existing people data with real-time updates, including dotted-line relationships and open roles.

features.title: Employee profiles
features.description: Customizable profiles showing skills, interests, pronouns, languages, and work experience.

features.title: Search and directory
features.description: Lets employees search, filter, and find colleagues by role, skill, or work experience.

features.title: Pages
features.description: Content organization capability within the platform.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: 13 prebuilt organizational analytics reports on the Directory plan.

features.title: API access
features.description: Developer API access available on the Directory and higher plans.

pros: 14-day free trial with full platform access
Serves 500,000+ employees across client organizations
Integrates with Microsoft Teams and HRIS/identity systems
Rated 4.8/5 on G2 and 4.6/5 on Capterra
Volume, long-term, and 15% nonprofit discounts available
cons: Minimum of 20 users required on the base plan
Additional per-user fees apply on top of the base monthly price
Enterprise plan requires custom pricing for organizations with 1,000+ employees
Founding year and company size are not disclosed

faqs.question: Does Sift offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sift offers a 14-day free trial with full platform access.

faqs.question: How much does Sift cost?
faqs.answer: Sift Org Chart starts at $150/month paid annually, Directory starts at $175/month, and Sift Complete starts at $200/month, each with additional per-user fees and a 20-user minimum.

faqs.question: Where is Sift based?
faqs.answer: Sift is headquartered in Detroit, Michigan.

faqs.question: Does Sift have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sift offers iOS and Android apps.

use_cases.title: Automated org chart maintenance
use_cases.description: HR teams keep org charts current automatically instead of manually updating slides or spreadsheets.

use_cases.title: Finding the right colleague
use_cases.description: Employees search the directory by skill or role to find the right person for a project.
use_cases.audience: Employees at mid-size and enterprise organizations

features: Combines Org Chart and Directory capabilities

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sift Org Chart?
paragraphs: Sift Org Chart is an organizational visibility platform, headquartered in Detroit, that automatically builds org charts and employee directories from a company's existing people data.
Plans start at $150/month for Org Chart, $175/month for Directory, and $200/month for the combined Sift Complete, each billed annually with a 20-user minimum and a 14-day free trial.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sift Org Chart is for
paragraphs: Sift serves mid-sized and enterprise organizations, including companies like Sumitomo, Rocket Companies, Grant Thornton, and Ping Identity, that want employees to easily find colleagues and understand company structure.

enrichment.short_description: Knowledge base and documentation software with a built-in AI chatbot, plans starting at $100/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: KnowledgeOwl is knowledge base software with a closed-system AI chatbot, white-glove migration, and plans from $100/month.

features.title: Unlimited nested categories
features.description: Organize articles with drag-and-drop category reorganization.

features.title: Built-in AI chatbot
features.description: A closed-system chatbot that only answers using content from your knowledge base and shows its sources.

features.title: White-glove migration
features.description: Assisted migration from platforms like Confluence, Zendesk, and SharePoint.

features.title: Custom theme design
features.description: Branding and theme customization for the knowledge base.

features.title: Analytics and deflection tracking
features.description: Built-in analytics with reader reports, ranging from 45 to 450+ days of data depending on plan.

features.title: Role-based access controls
features.description: Controls who can view or edit specific content.

features.title: Version history
features.description: Tracks document revisions over time.

pros: Closed-system AI chatbot only answers from your own content and cites its sources
White-glove migration and theme build included on every paid plan
1:1 onboarding calls included, more frequent on higher tiers
Supports GDPR and HIPAA compliance needs
In business for 11+ years
cons: No free tier; entry plan starts at $100/month
Article limits apply per plan (1,000 on Basic, 2,500 on Pro, 5,000 on Business)
AI credits are capped by plan (500 on Pro, 1,000 on Business) rather than unlimited
Additional authors ($25/month) and knowledge bases ($50/month) cost extra
Headquarters and founding year are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does KnowledgeOwl have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, KnowledgeOwl's plans start at $100/month for the Basic tier; there is no free tier.

faqs.question: How does KnowledgeOwl's AI chatbot work?
faqs.answer: It is a closed-system AI chatbot that only answers using content from your own knowledge base, always shows its sources, and does not use your data to train external LLMs.

faqs.question: What platforms can KnowledgeOwl migrate from?
faqs.answer: KnowledgeOwl offers white-glove migration from platforms including Confluence, Zendesk, and SharePoint.

faqs.question: How long has KnowledgeOwl been around?
faqs.answer: KnowledgeOwl states it has been in business for 11+ years.

use_cases.title: Replacing scattered internal docs
use_cases.description: Teams consolidate internal and external documentation that was previously spread across Confluence, SharePoint, or shared drives.
use_cases.audience: IT, support, and knowledge management teams

use_cases.title: Customer self-service with AI answers
use_cases.description: Support teams deploy the built-in AI chatbot so customers get sourced answers directly from published documentation.

features: AI chatbot, search, and article creation
90 days of data plus reader reports
450 days of data plus reader reports

long_form_blocks.heading: What is KnowledgeOwl?
paragraphs: KnowledgeOwl is knowledge base and documentation software that lets teams create and share internal and external documentation from one platform. It includes a closed-system AI chatbot that only answers using content from the customer's own knowledge base and cites its sources.

Paid plans start at $100/month for the Basic tier and scale up to Business at $500/month, with an Enterprise tier offering unlimited articles, AI credits, and analytics. Every paid plan includes white-glove migration and a custom theme build.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who KnowledgeOwl is for
paragraphs: KnowledgeOwl targets organizations across healthcare, energy, financial services, education, manufacturing, IT services, and call centers that want to move off Confluence, SharePoint, or scattered documents into a dedicated knowledge base.
